summary: Excellent audience tape of a high‑energy 1971 show, highlighted by a scorching Hard to Handle and seamless Truckin’ → St Stephen transition.
review: Fans rave about the electrifying performance – especially the blistering Hard to Handle, killer Truckin' segue into St. Stephen and the psychedelic Other One stretches – all captured on an surprisingly clear audience recording. The tape’s vintage lo‑fi charm adds to the excitement, and even though Jerry’s guitar was a touch out of tune at times, his playing still shines.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews are 4‑5 stars with consistent praise for both the show’s energy and the surprisingly good recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly note how “sick” and clear the tape sounds despite being a 1971 audience recording; no major complaints about drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High energy throughout with seamless transitions (e.g., Truckin' → St. Stephen, Other One → M&MU → Other One) that keep the momentum rolling.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar was noted as slightly out of tune in one spot but still delivered an incredible tone and drive.
